skill.name: track-management
skill.description: Guide AI coding assistants through Conductor track creation, planning, lifecycle updates, and completion workflows.
skill.summary: Manage Conductor tracks with clear specs, plans, status markers, and lifecycle records.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown examples and ordinary planning text only. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or network behavior were found in the reviewed files. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain Markdown inline code, fenced examples, file names, status markers, and slash-command references. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or other external commands, and no user-controlled command path is present. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are documentation only. Each flagged location is Markdown syntax or a written example, not runnable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations are normal words, requirement placeholders, or references to commit SHAs for traceability. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended by the skill. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is planning documentation and example templates. There is no hash function call, encryption routine, or security-sensitive cryptographic guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern in Checklist Heading
low_findings.description: The flagged line is part of a plan quality checklist about completeness. No network scanning command, host discovery, port scan, or remote endpoint appears in context. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation heading and nearby checklist content. There is no operational network behavior or instruction.

content.value_statement: Large coding tasks are hard to scope, plan, and track across multiple files. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create clear Conductor tracks with specs, plans, markers, and completion records.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to create feature, bug, chore, or refactor tracks.
Defines track ID, spec.md, plan.md, tracks.md, and metadata conventions.
Provides templates for specifications, implementation plans, and track registry entries.
Guides status marker use for pending, in-progress, complete, skipped, and blocked tasks.
Offers quality checklists for requirements, scope, dependencies, risks, tasks, and phases.
Describes lifecycle steps for creating, implementing, completing, and reverting tracks.
limitations: It provides documentation patterns, not an executable track management system.
It assumes the project already uses Conductor-style track files and commands.
It does not verify Git history or commit SHAs automatically.
It does not replace team review for scope, priority, and checkpoint approval.

use_cases.title: Create a Feature Track
use_cases.description: Turn a product request into a scoped track with requirements, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and phased implementation tasks.

use_cases.title: Organize a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Capture reproduction steps, failing tests, fix tasks, verification steps, and completion records for a defect.

use_cases.title: Review Track Quality
use_cases.description: Check that a spec and plan have clear scope, testable requirements, realistic phases, and useful status markers.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the track-management skill to create a Conductor track for this request: [describe the feature, bug, chore, or refactor]. Ask me for any missing scope, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the track-management skill to draft a spec.md for this track: [track idea]. Include overview, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, acceptance criteria, scope, dependencies, risks, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Implementation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the track-management skill to create a phased plan.md from this spec: [paste or summarize spec]. Include tasks, verification steps, checkpoints, and status marker guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Track Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the track-management skill to review this existing track for readiness: [paste spec and plan summaries]. Identify missing requirements, unclear scope, weak verification, dependency gaps, and status marker issues.

output_examples.input: Create a track for adding account lockout after repeated failed login attempts.
output_examples.output: A concise feature track proposal with a date-based track ID, security-focused requirements, acceptance criteria, dependencies, risks, and a three-phase implementation plan.

output_examples.input: Review this plan for a refactor track before implementation starts.
output_examples.output: A readiness review that flags oversized phases, missing verification tasks, unclear rollback needs, and specific updates for plan.md.

output_examples.input: Help update status markers after phase one is complete.
output_examples.output: A set of marker updates for completed tasks, the current phase checkpoint, blocked work, and the tracks.md registry entry.

best_practices: Keep each track focused on one coherent change with clear completion criteria.
Write testable acceptance criteria before creating detailed implementation tasks.
Update task markers, checkpoint SHAs, and registry status as work progresses.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated features, bugs, and refactors into one track.
Do not start implementation from a vague spec with unresolved scope questions.
Do not mark phases complete without verification tasks and checkpoint records.

faq.question: What is a Conductor track?
faq.answer: A Conductor track is a logical work unit for a feature, bug, chore, or refactor. It groups a spec, plan, metadata, and status records.

faq.question: Does this skill create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates that an AI assistant can use when creating or updating track files.

faq.question: Which files does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on spec.md, plan.md, tracks.md, metadata.json, and track index files.

faq.question: Can it manage bug fix work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a bug fix pattern with reproduction, fix, and verification phases.

faq.question: How does it handle blocked work?
faq.answer: It recommends blocked markers, metadata updates, registry updates, and dependency tracking for unresolved work.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are documentation only and contain no executable scripts, network behavior, or prompt injection attempts.
